**Li Guixian** ([Chinese](/wiki/Simplified_Chinese_characters "Simplified Chinese characters"): 李贵鲜; born August 1937) is a retired [politician](/wiki/Politician "Politician") of the [People's Republic of China](/wiki/People%27s_Republic_of_China "People's Republic of China"), and a governor of [People's Bank of China](/wiki/People%27s_Bank_of_China "People's Bank of China") in 1988–1993.[[1]](#cite_note-chinavitae-1)

Li is a native of [Gaizhou](/wiki/Gaizhou "Gaizhou"), [Liaoning Province](/wiki/Liaoning_Province "Liaoning Province"). He studied at the [Chinese University of Science and Technology](/wiki/Chinese_University_of_Science_and_Technology "Chinese University of Science and Technology") in 1959–1960. Next, he studied at the [Mendeleev Chemical Technology Institute in Moscow](/wiki/D._Mendeleev_University_of_Chemical_Technology_of_Russia "D. Mendeleev University of Chemical Technology of Russia") starting in 1960, graduating in 1965.[[1]](#cite_note-chinavitae-1)

Li joined the [Chinese Communist Party](/wiki/Chinese_Communist_Party "Chinese Communist Party") in 1962. He became the secretary of the CCP Liaoning Committee in 1985,[[2]](#cite_note-2) and secretary of the CCP [Anhui](/wiki/Anhui "Anhui") Committee in 1986. In 1988–1993, he served as the governor of the People's Bank of China.
